title: Sound travels about four times faster underwater than it does through air — which is why whale songs can travel hundreds of miles across the ocean, and why early submarine sonar operators sometimes picked up the calls of distant whales communicating from hundreds of miles away
description: Sound in air travels at about 343 metres per second, the figure that fighter pilots cross when they break the sound barrier. Sound in seawater travels at about 1,500 metres per second. The ratio is roughly 4.3 to 1. The reason is straightforward physics: soun…
content: Sound in air travels at about 343 metres per second, the figure that fighter pilots cross when they break the sound barrier. Sound in seawater travels at about 1,500 metres per second. The ratio is r… [+6789 chars]

Schall breitet sich in der Luft mit etwa 343 Metern pro Sekunde aus, der Wert, den Kampfpiloten überschreiten, wenn sie die Schallmauer durchbrechen. Schall breitet sich im Meerwasser mit etwa 1.500 Metern pro Sekunde aus. Das Verhältnis beträgt ungefähr 4,3 zu 1. Der Grund ist einfache Physik: Schall…
en: Sound travels about four times faster underwater than it does through air — which is why whale songs can travel hundreds of miles across the ocean, and why early submarine sonar operators sometimes picked up the calls of distant whales communicating from hundreds of miles away. The ratio is roughly 4.3 to 1. The reason is straightforward physics: soun…

Sonus in aere perambulat circiter 343 metris per secundam, figuram transeuntem gubernatores pugnantis cum obice sonum frangunt. Sonus in marinis percurrit circiter 1.500 metra per secundam. title: Nuclear shell structure governs short-range nucleon pairing
description: The scattering of high-energy electrons from three different nuclei demonstrated that short-range-correlated pairing depends far more on the specific quantum orbitals occupied by nucleons than predicted by theoretical models.
content:
<li>Hen, O., Miller, G. A., Piasetzky, E. & Weinstein, L. B. Nucleonnucleon correlations, short-lived excitations, and the quarks within. Rev. Mod. Phys.89, 045002 (2017).
Article 
 ADS 
Googl… [+5626 chars]
de: Die Struktur der Kernhülle bestimmt die Nukleonenpaarung im Nahbereich. Die Streuung hochenergetischer Elektronen aus drei verschiedenen Kernen zeigte, dass die kurzreichweitenkorrelierte Paarung weitaus stärker von den spezifischen Quantenorbitalen abhängt, die von Nukleonen besetzt werden, als durch theoretische Modelle vorhergesagt.
en: Nuclear shell structure governs short-range nucleon pairing. The scattering of high-energy electrons from three different nuclei demonstrated that short-range-correlated pairing depends far more on the specific quantum orbitals occupied by nucleons than predicted by theoretical models.
la: Concha nuclei structurae breves nuclei connubium regit. Disiectio summae energiae ex tribus diversis nucleis demonstratum est, connubium breve connectivum longe magis a speciebus quantis orbitalibus a nucleis occupatis quam a exemplaribus theoreticis praedictum.

title: The coldest capital city in the world is not in Russia, Scandinavia, or Canada — it’s Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia, which has an annual average temperature of -1.3°C (29.7°F), the only national capital with a sub-zero annual average, and where January nighttime temperatures regularly drop to -35°C (-31°F), making it colder than Moscow, Reykjavík, Astana, and Ottawa combined

Ulaanbaatar, die Hauptstadt der Mongolei, liegt in einem Hochtal am Fluss Tuul im Norden der Zentralmongolei, auf einer Höhe von etwa 1.300 Metern und einer Breite von etwa 47 Grad nördlicher Breite. Die Jahresdurchschnittstemperatur liegt bei etwa -1,3 °C (29,7 °F).
